front: How do you deploy Dagster on AWS ECS with Terraform?
back:
Dagster can be deployed on ECS Fargate as three services: webserver (Dagit), daemon, and code locations (user code). Terraform provisions the full stack.

High-level architecture:
```
ECS Cluster
  dagster-webserver (Dagit UI)  -> ALB -> internet
  dagster-daemon                -> background scheduler/sensor
  dagster-user-code             -> your pipeline code
RDS PostgreSQL                  -> Dagster run storage
S3 bucket                       -> compute logs
```

Key Terraform resources:
```hcl
resource "aws_ecs_cluster" "dagster" {
  name = "dagster-prod"
}

resource "aws_ecs_task_definition" "webserver" {
  family                   = "dagster-webserver"
  requires_compatibilities = ["FARGATE"]
  network_mode             = "awsvpc"
  cpu                      = 1024
  memory                   = 2048
  execution_role_arn       = aws_iam_role.ecs_execution.arn
  task_role_arn            = aws_iam_role.dagster_task.arn

  container_definitions = jsonencode([{
    name  = "webserver"
    image = "dagster/dagster-celery-k8s:1.6.0"
    environment = [
      { name = "DAGSTER_PG_HOST", value = aws_db_instance.dagster.address },
      { name = "DAGSTER_PG_DB",   value = "dagster" }
    ]
    portMappings = [{ containerPort = 3000, protocol = "tcp" }]
    logConfiguration = {
      logDriver = "awslogs"
      options = {
        "awslogs-group"  = "/ecs/dagster"
        "awslogs-region" = "us-east-1"
      }
    }
  }])
}
```

DE tip: For simpler ops, Dagster Cloud (SaaS) eliminates all this infrastructure. Use self-hosted ECS when data must stay in your VPC.
